mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.82% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns in easing periods fail to exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.0% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that presages lower corporate earnings growth. This interaction effect produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because both demand channels are simultaneously impaired.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 501971M and HOUST at 1350K provide baseline
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression against direction_20d.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and signals reduced economic momentum. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 5-day forward returns in deceleration periods do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K month-over-month, signaling robust labor market tightness that supports consumer income growth and discretionary spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: tight labor market or expansion phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, real rates are negative and compressed, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 250.857 and FEDFUNDS at 1.82%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predicting direction_20d.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, indicating weakening economic momentum that presages lower corporate earnings and equity valuations. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1.4M annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow > 0.5% month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand strength across residential and consumer channels. This joint expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.4M annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1350K and RSAFS at 501971M
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion or mid-cycle acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
